sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Excellent! Clicking on the white container (row 4, col 37) changed 94 cells and the container in the main area is now WHITE instead of purple! The selector also moved to under the white container. Now I have:
- White container (instead of purple)
- Purple block below with diagonal white pattern
- White block below that
Now I need to use SPACE with the white container to stamp white onto the blocks. The goal is white on top, purple on bottom. Currently the blocks have:
- Top 5 rows: purple
- Bottom 5 rows: diagonal purple/white mix
I need to stamp white on the top 5 rows and purple on the bottom 5 rows. Let me try SPACE with the white container to see if it stamps white.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Now with white container selected, try SPACE to stamp white
action(['SPACE'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
